content: Universal Context Engine (UCE) Introduction Universal Context Engine (UCE) is an AI-powered platform designed to understand documents, retrieve knowledge, answer user questions, and execute actions across connected applications. The system combines semantic search, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), vector databases, and intelligent orchestration to create an interactive AI workspace. The primary AI assistant is named Alex. Alex helps users search knowledge, summarize information, answer questions, and interact with external tools. The system focuses on creating a centralized intelligence layer capable of understanding both structured and unstructured information. System Architecture The architecture consists of several major components. User Interface:  Next.js  TypeScript  Tailwind

architecture consists of several major components. User Interface:  Next.js  TypeScript  Tailwind CSS  Framer Motion Backend:  FastAPI  Python  REST APIs Knowledge Layer:  Document Parser  Text Chunking  Embedding Generation  ChromaDB Vector Database AI Layer:  Semantic Retrieval  Context Pipeline  AI Orchestrator  Response Service Tool Layer:  Email Tool  Task Manager  Future MCP Integrations Backend Technologies The backend is built using FastAPI. FastAPI provides:  High performance  Automatic API documentation  Async request handling  Easy dependency injection  Strong typing Python powers all AI services including embedding generation, semantic retrieval, and orchestration. The backend exposes REST endpoints for document upload, chat, retrieval, and tool execution

tration. The backend exposes REST endpoints for document upload, chat, retrieval, and tool execution. Frontend Technologies The frontend uses:  Next.js  React  TypeScript  Tailwind CSS  shadcn/ui  Framer Motion The interface is designed as an AI Workspace where Alex assists users through conversations, document management, and connected applications. Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) The platform uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Pipeline: 1. Upload document 2. Extract text 3. Split into chunks 4. Generate embeddings 5. Store embeddings in ChromaDB 6. Perform semantic search 7. Retrieve relevant chunks 8. Build context 9. Generate final response This approach improves factual accuracy by grounding AI responses in uploaded documents. Connected Applications The system supports co

uracy by grounding AI responses in uploaded documents. Connected Applications The system supports connected tools. Current:  Email  Task Manager Future Integrations:  GitHub  Slack  Google Calendar  Notion  WhatsApp  Microsoft Teams The architecture is compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling AI-driven tool execution. Future Roadmap Upcoming improvements include:  Multi-agent collaboration  Multiple LLM providers  API key rotation  Voice interaction  OCR for scanned PDFs  Knowledge graphs  Fine-grained permissions  Multi-user workspaces  Enterprise authentication  Analytics dashboard
